episode.title: Flour spilled at 4AM — something walked through it

shots.caption: Every morning the bakers found flour on the floor. The bag was always still sealed.
shots.prompt: The bakery prep kitchen sits spotless and still in the same fixed view: steel worktables, stacked bowls, paper flour sacks leaning by a table leg, one dim safety light over the door. Nothing moves. Ends on: the exact same still kitchen.

In the same fixed view, the tallest paper flour sack slowly leans away from the table leg and tips over; a wide fan of white flour spreads across the dark tiled floor and the fine dust settles. Ends on: a smooth, untouched fan of flour on the floor.

In the same fixed view, small bare footprints press into the smooth flour ONE BY ONE, appearing out of nothing, walking away from the camera across the spill — each print sharp and child-sized, with no one there to make them. Ends on: a trail of small footprints crossing the flour.

In the same fixed view, the trail of footprints simply STOPS in the middle of the open floor. On the far worktable a metal mixing bowl rocks and spins slowly to a stop, and the door under the safety light sways gently once. Ends on: the still kitchen, prints ending mid-floor.

shots.caption: The footprints ended in the middle of the room. They never walked back.
shots.prompt: In the same fixed view, the kitchen is silent: the tipped sack, the fan of flour and the trail of small footprints all exactly as they were, while a thin strip of early morning light appears under the door. Ends on: the still kitchen holding its evidence, morning approaching.
